AddressDomain.description: Domain name of the connection address

AddressIP.description:
IP corresponding to the connection address

:::tip
It is not recommended to connect to the instance using the `IP:port` format, as the IP address may change after switching subnets. If the client code is not updated in time, instance connection failures may occur. We recommend accessing the MongoDB instance using the `domain:port` format to avoid connection issues caused by IP address changes. For detailed steps to connect to a MongoDB instance, see [Connect to an Instance Using the Mongo Shell Tool](../6447/70381).
:::

AddressPort.description: Port number of the connection address

AddressType.description:
Connection address type of the node. Possible values:

Primary: Primary node connection address
Secondary: Secondary node connection address
Hidden: Hidden node connection address
ReadOnly: Read-only node connection address

:::tip
Hidden node connection address information is returned only when the connection address type is a private connection address (that is, when `NetworkType` is set to `Private`).
:::

EipId.description:
EIP ID bound to the instance's public address

:::tip
This parameter is returned only when the connection address type is a public connection address (that is, when `NetworkType` is set to `Public`).
:::

NodeId.description: Node ID associated with the connection address

ZoneId.description: AZ of the node; all nodes are consistent for single-AZ instances, while nodes differ for multi-AZ instances

EipIds.description: List of EIP IDs to bind when applying for a public address

EndpointStr.description: String information of the connection address.

EndpointType.description:
Node type corresponding to the connection address. Possible values:

- Replica set instances only support the `ReplicaSet` (replica set) connection address type
- Sharded cluster instances support three connection address types: `Mongos` (Mongos node), `ConfigServer` (ConfigServer node), and `Shard` (Shard node)

InstanceId.description:
Instance ID

:::tip
You can call the [DescribeDBInstances](https://www.volcengine.com/docs/6447/100741) API to query basic information for all MongoDB instances in the target region, including the instance ID.
:::

MongosNodeIds.description: ID of the Mongos node that requires a public connection address

NetworkType.description:
Network type of the connection address. Possible values:

- `Private`: Private connection address
- `Public`: Public connection address

ObjectId.description:
ID of the component associated with the current connection address

:::tip
This parameter is returned only when `EndpointType` is set to `Mongos`, `ConfigServer`, or `Shard`.
:::

SubnetId.description: Subnet ID of the current instance

VpcId.description: VPC ID of the current instance
